methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
Enapter is a public-stage climate and energy company founded by Sebastian-Justus Schmidt. The valuation exercise examines a universe of 9 comparable companies across the Climate & Energy sector: 1 public peer (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 private peers including QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient comparable depth. While 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy broadly, application of stage-specific and valuation-tier filters yielded fewer than three datapoints in any narrow cohort. Specifically, the filtered peer set contained only 2 same-stage comparables and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier peers meeting minimum thresholds. Falling back to all-sector median multiples risked material misalignment with Enapter's specific capital intensity, technology maturity, and commercialization profile.

This analysis does not yield a triangulated valuation range. The constraint on peer density within defensible filter criteria prevents application of the primary analytical method. Any valuation conclusion would require either: expansion of the comparison universe to less-adjacent energy or industrial sectors; reliance on transaction multiples or precedent financings from the private peer set (data not provided); or DCF-based analysis anchored to company-specific unit economics and production capacity projections. The key uncertainty remains the availability of sufficient stage and sector-aligned comparables to establish robust market-based valuation support.
